plan_steps.action: Search the entire codebase (source files, configs, queries, APIs) for any usage of 'pageindex' — case-insensitive — including variable names, query parameters, function arguments, and pagination logic.

Analyze the findings from step_1. Determine: (1) whether 'pageindex' is actively used or deprecated, (2) which modules/components use it, (3) whether it is consistent with any pagination standard (0-based vs 1-based), and (4) if there are any alternatives already in use (e.g., 'page', 'offset', 'cursor').
If 'pageindex' is found in step_1, review existing tests that cover pagination logic. Identify gaps — specifically whether 'pageindex' boundary cases (e.g., 0, negative, out-of-range) are tested.

Based on the analysis in step_2, evaluate whether the current use of 'pageindex' aligns with best practices for pagination design (REST standards, GraphQL cursor-based, etc.). Recommend whether to keep, rename, or replace it.
